title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "North San Diego County has struggled in years past to get its fair share of resources.",
    "The 2019-20 budget includes $1 million towards homelessness prevention for several North San Diego cities.",
    "Significant investments targeted at homelessness prevention have been allocated from the new state budget.",
    "The Veterans Resource Center at Mira Costa College received $1.5 million to support former military transitioning into the workforce.",
    "$8 million has been allocated for beachfront improvements in the City of Oceanside to strengthen public safety and revitalize the area.",
    "$17.5 million in additional funding has been allocated to support Senior Nutrition Programs.",
    "$4.5 million in new funding has been secured for local grants to support the Healthy Brain Initiative that improves Alzheimer’s disease diagnosis and prevention.",
    "$1 million has been allocated to support a gun violence reduction pilot program through the San Diego County Sheriff’s Department.",
    "$5 million has been designated for the Youth Reinvestment Grant to strengthen partnerships for at-risk youth diversion programs.",
    "$5 million has been allocated for research and projects on climate change and its impacts on coastal erosion through the California Department of Parks and Recreation.",
    "$2 million in grant funds have been allocated to support Special Olympics programs.",
    "$5 million has been allocated for Hepatitis C monitoring, prevention, and testing activities.",
    "$50 million in additional support has been secured for the After School Education and Safety Program.",
    "The budget leaves $19.4 billion in reserves, including $16.5 billion for a rainy day fund."
  ]
}
```
